revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Jingus without interwikis]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24593703458|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Yamaguchi Prefecture, Japan|qq=}} {{more citations needed|date=August 2016|qq=}}{{Infobox religious building | name = Akama Shrine
赤間神宮 | image = Akama jingu.JPG | caption = ''Torii'' of Akama Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|33|57|35|N|130|56|54.5|E|region:JP_scale:30000_source:jawiki|display=title,inline|qq=}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Imperial Shrine | deity = {{ill|Emperor Antoku|en|Emperor Antoku|qid=Q316651|lt=Emperor Antoku|1=Emperor Antoku}} | founded_by = | established = 1191 | date_destroyed = | location = 4-1, Amidaiji-chō
{{ill|Shimonoseki|en|Shimonoseki|qid=Q203308|lt=Shimonoseki|1=Shimonoseki}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|en|Yamaguchi Prefecture|qid=Q127264|lt=Yamaguchi|1=Yamaguchi Prefecture}} 750-0003 | website = {{URL|http://www.tiki.ne.jp/~akama-jingu/|qq=}}{{in lang|ja|qq=}}{{dead link|date=August 2019|qq=}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} '''{{nihongo|Akama Shrine|赤間神宮|Akama Jingū|qq=}}''' is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|Shimonoseki|en|Shimonoseki|qid=Q203308|lt=Shimonoseki|1=Shimonoseki}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|en|Yamaguchi Prefecture|qid=Q127264|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|1=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It is dedicated to the child {{ill|Emperor Antoku|en|Emperor Antoku|qid=Q316651|lt=Emperor Antoku|1=Emperor Antoku}}, who died in the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|en|Battle of Dan-no-ura|qid=Q968676|lt=Battle of Dan-no-ura|1=Battle of Dan-no-ura}} (aka Dannoura), which occurred nearby in 1185. This battle was important in the history of Japan because it brought an end to {{ill|Genpei War|en|Genpei War|qid=Q737542|lt=Genpei War|1=Genpei War}} in which the [[Minamoto clan]] defeated the rival [[Taira clan]], and ended the Taira bid for control of Japan. The shrine is situated on the waterfront of the [[:en:Kanmon Strait|Kanmon Strait]], between the centre of Shimonoseki and the tourist restaurants of {{ill|Karato|en|Karato|qid=Q6368794|lt=Karato|1=Karato}}, Kanmon Wharf. The bright red main gate makes it a very visible sight. ==Description== The colours and style of the gate are inspired by [[Ryūgū-jō]], according to the Shimonoseki Tourist GuidebookShimonoseki Tourist Guidebook, a pamphlet published by the Shimonoseki City Tourism Department, March 2007, in Japanese. published by the Shimonoseki City Tourism Department. This source states that Antoku's grandmother, [[:en:Nii-no-Ama|Nii-no-Ama]], who drowned with Antoku, wished for their palace to be created underwater as she jumped into the sea. Ryūgū-jō is a mythical underwater palace, belonging to the [[Ryūjin|dragon god of the sea]]. In the [[:en:Tale of Heike|Tale of Heike]], Nii-no-Ama told Antoku, before jumping, that they would go to an underwater palace, without referring to Ryūgū-jō. Then, Antoku's mother (Kenreimon-In, aka [[Taira no Tokuko]]) had a dream, in which they were living in Ryūgū-jō. Inside, in the Hoichi Hall, is a statue of {{ill|Hoichi the Earless|en|Hoichi the Earless|qid=Q1057883|lt=Hoichi the Earless|1=Hoichi the Earless}}, one of the characters in a traditional ghost story which was made known in the west by {{ill|Lafcadio Hearn|en|Lafcadio Hearn|qid=Q313638|lt=Lafcadio Hearn|1=Lafcadio Hearn}}. The grounds also contain the Nanamori-zuka (seven mounds), which represent the Heike warriors also lost in the Battle of Dan-no-Ura. ==Kanpei-sha== In 1871, the {{nihongo|''Kanpei-sha''|官幣社|qq=}} identified the hierarchy of government-supported shrines most closely associated with the Imperial family.Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' p. 124. The ''kampeisha'' were shrines venerated by the imperial family. This kk encompasses those sanctuaries enshrining emperors, imperial family members, or meritorious retainers of the Imperial family.Institute for Japanese Culture and Classics, Kokugakuin University: [http://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/glossary/def_K.html Glossary of Shinto Names and Terms, ''Kampei Taisha.''] Up through 1940, the mid-range of Ranked Imperial shrines or {{nihongo|''[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Kanpei-chūsha]]''|官幣中社|qq=}} included the shrine; and it was then known as Akama''-gū''Ponsonby-Fane. ''Imperial,'' p. 125. In 1940, Akama's status was changed {{nihongo|''Kanpei-taisha''|官幣大社|qq=}}, which is the highest Rank; and since then, it has been known as Akama ''jingū.''Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1963). ''The Viscissitudes of Shinto,'' p. 394. ==See also== * {{ill|List of Jingū|en|List of Jingū|qid=Q120680118|lt=List of Jingū|1=List of Jingū}} * [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] ==Notes== {{reflist|qq=}} ==References== * [http://www.jnto.go.jp/eng/arrange/attractions/facilities/shrines_temples/83dn3a000000ejob.html Japan National Tourist Organisation] (note that the information that Emperor Antoku was three years old when he died is contradicted by other sources, which say that although he was a young child, he was a few years older than that. See the Wikipedia page for [[:en:Antoku|Antoku]]) * {{ill|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|en|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|qid=Q3246771|lt=Ponsonby-Fane|1=Richard Ponsonby-Fane}}, Richard Arthur Brabazon. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [http://www.worldcat.org/wcpa/oclc/194887 OCLC 194887] * _______________. (1962). [https://books.google.com/books?id=tjEvAAAAYAAJ&q=Studies+in+Shinto+and+Shrines ''Studies in Shinto and Shrines.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [http://www.worldcat.org/wcpa/oclc/3994492 OCLC 399449] * _______________. (1963). [https://books.google.com/books?id=Uru8GMeblXgC&q=sapporo+shrine+yoshihisa-shinno ''The Viciissitudes of Shinto.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. {{OCLC|186605327|qq=}} {{Shinto shrine|qq=}}{{Beppyo Shrines|qq=}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Jingū]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Kanpei Chūsha]] [[Category:Shimonoseki]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] }} [[image:Akama-jingu suiten-mon 2.jpeg|right|thumb|Suiten mon, main gate of the shrine]] [[image:Nanamori-zuka.jpeg|right|thumb|Nanamori-zuka, tomb of Taira clan warriors]] [[en:Akama Jingu]] [[en:Akama Shrine]] [[en:Akama Jingu]] [[Category:Pages without wikidata]]
